overview.tagline: A family-friendly suburban township with excellent schools and a welcoming community
overview.description: The Township of Wyckoff is a residential suburb in northwestern Bergen County known for its outstanding public schools, safe neighborhoods, and strong sense of community. With a charming town center, scenic parks, and convenient access to both New York City and the New Jersey countryside, Wyckoff offers an ideal balance of suburban tranquility and metropolitan accessibility.
overview.schoolDistrict: Wyckoff Public Schools (K-8) / Ramapo Indian Hills Regional High School District (9-12)
highlights: Top-rated K-8 school district and highly ranked regional high schools
Wyckoff town center with local shops and restaurants
Beautiful residential neighborhoods with mature landscaping
James A. McFaul Environmental Center for nature education
Strong community events and recreation programs

realEstate.assessmentInfo: Property assessments managed through the Bergen County Tax Board. Wyckoff properties are assessed at a percentage of market value with periodic revaluations.
realEstate.recentTrends: Wyckoff continues to see strong demand from families attracted by the school district. The market is competitive with limited inventory. Homes in desirable neighborhoods near schools and the town center sell quickly, often above asking price.
neighborhoodNotes: Wyckoff Town Center — Walking distance to shops, restaurants, and community amenities
Sicomac area — Established neighborhood with proximity to Sicomac Elementary
Clinton Avenue corridor — Spacious properties with a mix of colonials and split-levels
Crescent Avenue area — Quiet, tree-lined streets popular with families
Russell Avenue neighborhood — Close to Eisenhower Middle School and parks

trashCollection.title: Trash Collection
trashCollection.description: Curbside trash pickup is provided by a contracted private hauler on behalf of the Township.
details: Twice-weekly pickup on designated days based on neighborhood zone
Trash must be placed curbside by 6:00 AM on collection day
Use standard garbage cans or bags; maximum 50 lbs per container
Bulk item pickup available by scheduling with the hauler
Holiday schedule adjustments announced by the Township

recycling.title: Recycling
recycling.description: Curbside recycling is collected on a weekly basis by the contracted hauler.
details: Single-stream recycling: glass, cans, plastics #1-#7, paper, and cardboard
Recycling must be placed in designated bins provided by the hauler
Yard waste collected seasonally from spring through fall
Leaf collection provided curbside during fall months
Special recycling drop-off events for electronics and hazardous waste

waterSewer.title: Water & Sewer
waterSewer.description: Water service is provided by the Wyckoff Water Department. Sewer service is managed by the Township.
details: Water sourced from local wells and supplemented by regional sources
Quarterly billing for water and sewer service
Annual water quality report available from the Wyckoff Water Department
Water conservation programs and rebates available
24/7 emergency response for water main breaks

permits.title: Building & Permits
permits.description: The Construction Department issues building permits and conducts inspections for all construction activity in the Township.
details: Permits required for new construction, renovations, additions, and pools
Zoning applications handled by the Planning Board and Zoning Board of Adjustment
Online permit application portal available
Inspections scheduled through the Construction Office
Fence, deck, and shed permits required per Township code

parking.title: Parking
parking.description: The Township maintains public parking areas in the town center and near public facilities.
details: Free parking in the Wyckoff town center municipal lots
Commuter parking available with permit at designated lots
No overnight parking on Township streets without permission
Parking enforcement by the Wyckoff Police Department

additionalServices.title: Wyckoff Public Library
additionalServices.description: Community library offering books, media, digital resources, and programs for all ages.
details: Located at 200 Woodland Avenue
Children's story times, summer reading program, and teen events
Part of the Bergen County Cooperative Library System (BCCLS)
Meeting rooms available for community use

additionalServices.title: Recreation Department
additionalServices.description: Comprehensive recreation programs for Wyckoff residents of all ages.
details: Youth sports leagues including soccer, baseball, basketball, and lacrosse
Summer day camp programs
Adult fitness and wellness programs
Senior citizen programs and trips

noise.title: Noise Ordinance
noise.summary: Wyckoff enforces noise regulations to preserve the quiet residential character of the Township.
keyRules: Construction work prohibited before 7:00 AM and after 7:00 PM on weekdays
Saturday construction allowed 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM
No construction permitted on Sundays or holidays
Gas-powered leaf blowers restricted during certain hours
Unreasonable noise prohibited at all times; enforcement after 10:00 PM

propertyMaintenance.title: Property Maintenance
propertyMaintenance.summary: Property owners must maintain their properties to preserve neighborhood appearance and safety.
keyRules: Grass and weeds must be kept under 10 inches in height
Sidewalks must be cleared of snow and ice within 24 hours after a storm
Properties must be free of debris, junk, and abandoned vehicles
Fences may not exceed 6 feet in height in rear yards
Swimming pools must be enclosed by a fence with a self-closing, self-latching gate

pets.title: Pet Regulations
pets.summary: Wyckoff requires all dogs to be licensed and enforces leash and waste cleanup ordinances.
keyRules: All dogs 7 months and older must be licensed annually
Rabies vaccination required for licensing
Dogs must be on a leash when off the owner's property
Pet owners must immediately clean up pet waste on public and others' property
Barking dog complaints are investigated by the police and animal control

parking.title: Parking Regulations
parking.summary: On-street parking is regulated to ensure safety and traffic flow in residential areas.
keyRules: No overnight parking on Township streets between 2:00 AM and 6:00 AM
Vehicles must be parked in the direction of traffic
Snow emergency parking bans require vehicles off-street
Commercial vehicles over 4 tons may not park on residential streets
Violators subject to fines and possible towing

rentals.title: Rental Property Regulations
rentals.summary: Rental properties must comply with Township housing standards and registration requirements.
keyRules: Rental properties must be registered with the Township
Certificate of Occupancy or Habitability required for rental units
Landlords responsible for maintaining properties to code
Short-term vacation rentals are restricted in residential zones
Inspections may be required at change of tenancy

other.title: Fence Regulations
other.summary: Fences in Wyckoff are regulated by height, material, and placement.
keyRules: Maximum height of 4 feet in front yard areas
Maximum height of 6 feet in side and rear yards
Fence permit required before installation
Finished side of fence must face outward toward neighbors

other.title: Garage Sales
other.summary: Residents may hold garage or yard sales subject to permit requirements.
keyRules: Permit required from the Township Clerk's office
Limited to two sales per year per household
Sales may not exceed two consecutive days
Signs must be removed within 24 hours after the sale

schools.publicDistrict: Wyckoff Public Schools (K-8) / Ramapo Indian Hills Regional High School District (9-12)

publicSchools.name: Ramapo High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school serving Wyckoff, Franklin Lakes, and Oakland; strong academics and athletics

publicSchools.name: Indian Hills High School
publicSchools.notes: Regional high school in Oakland also serving Wyckoff students

publicSchools.name: Eisenhower Middle School
publicSchools.notes: The sole middle school in the Wyckoff district, serving all township students in grades 5-8

publicSchools.name: Lincoln Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Neighborhood elementary school in central Wyckoff

publicSchools.name: Sicomac Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the Sicomac section of the township

publicSchools.name: Coolidge Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school serving the western portion of Wyckoff

publicSchools.name: Washington Elementary School
publicSchools.notes: Elementary school in the southeastern section of Wyckoff

privateSchools.name: Eastern Christian School
privateSchools.notes: Christian school with a campus in Wyckoff offering PreK through grade 12; strong college preparatory program

privateSchools.name: St. Elizabeth School
privateSchools.notes: Catholic elementary school associated with St. Elizabeth parish

nearbyHigherEd: Ramapo College of New Jersey (Mahwah) — 15 minutes

whyMoveHere: Highly rated K-8 school district with strong performance metrics
Safe, family-oriented community with low crime rates
Convenient access to Route 208 and NJ-17 for commuting
Charming town center with local shops and dining
Beautiful residential neighborhoods with spacious lots
Proximity to outdoor recreation at Ramapo Valley County Reservation

commuteToNYC.primaryMethod: NJ Transit Bus or drive to train station
commuteToNYC.duration: 55-75 minutes to midtown Manhattan by bus
commuteToNYC.notes: Wyckoff does not have its own train station. Most commuters drive to the Ridgewood or Waldwick NJ Transit stations for train service, or take the 148/196 bus routes which connect to the Port Authority Bus Terminal in Manhattan. Drive to Ridgewood station is approximately 10 minutes.

costOfLiving.summary: Wyckoff is an affluent Bergen County suburb with a cost of living well above state and national averages, primarily due to housing costs and property taxes.
costOfLiving.vsStateAverage: Approximately 45% above the New Jersey state average

pros: Strong community spirit with active recreation programs
Good access to major highways (Route 208, NJ-17)
cons: High property taxes typical of Bergen County
No direct train service — must drive to nearby stations
Town center is small compared to neighboring Ridgewood

restaurants.name: Wyckoff Thai Kitchen
restaurants.description: Popular Thai restaurant serving authentic dishes including curries, noodles, and fresh seafood preparations.

restaurants.name: Biagio's Ristorante
restaurants.description: Upscale Italian dining with classic dishes, an extensive wine list, and a warm ambiance.

restaurants.name: The Brick House
restaurants.description: Casual American restaurant and bar known for burgers, craft beers, and a lively atmosphere.

restaurants.name: Wyckoff Bagels & Deli
restaurants.description: Local bagel shop serving fresh-baked bagels, breakfast sandwiches, and classic deli fare.

restaurants.name: Mezza
restaurants.description: Mediterranean and Middle Eastern cuisine featuring shawarma, falafel, hummus, and grilled specialties.

parks.name: James A. McFaul Environmental Center
parks.description: County-run nature center with 81 acres of trails, wildlife exhibits, and environmental education programs. A gem for nature lovers and families.

parks.name: Wyckoff Community Park
parks.description: Large township park featuring sports fields, playgrounds, basketball courts, and open green space for community activities.

parks.name: Pulis Park
parks.description: Neighborhood park with walking trails, a pond, playgrounds, and picnic areas in a peaceful wooded setting.

parks.name: Zabriskie Pond Park
parks.description: Scenic park built around a historic pond, offering fishing, walking paths, and wildlife viewing.

events.name: Wyckoff Day
events.description: Annual community celebration featuring carnival rides, food vendors, games, live entertainment, and fireworks.
events.when: September, typically a Saturday in mid-September

events.name: Wyckoff Farmers Market
events.description: Seasonal farmers market offering local produce, baked goods, flowers, and artisan products.

events.name: Holiday Tree Lighting
events.description: Annual holiday tree lighting ceremony in the town center with carols, hot cocoa, and a visit from Santa.

events.name: Environmental Center Nature Programs
events.description: Year-round nature walks, wildlife programs, and seasonal events at the McFaul Environmental Center.

attractions.name: James A. McFaul Environmental Center
attractions.description: Bergen County environmental education center with nature trails, wildlife gardens, and hands-on exhibits covering local ecology and conservation.

attractions.name: Wyckoff Town Center
attractions.description: Small but charming collection of local shops, restaurants, and services along Main Street and Wyckoff Avenue.
attractions.address: Main Street & Wyckoff Avenue, Wyckoff, NJ 07481

attractions.name: Ramapo Valley County Reservation
attractions.description: Nearby Bergen County park offering extensive hiking trails, fishing, and scenic views of the Ramapo Mountains. Located in adjacent Mahwah but a popular destination for Wyckoff residents.

shoppingAreas: Wyckoff Town Center — Local shops and restaurants along Main Street
Godwin Avenue — Additional retail and dining options
Paramus shopping — Major malls and retail centers 15 minutes away

government.meetingSchedule: Township Committee meets the 2nd and 4th Tuesday of each month at 7:30 PM
government.meetingLocation: Wyckoff Municipal Building, 340 Franklin Avenue, Wyckoff, NJ 07481

hospitals.name: Valley Hospital (Valley Health System)
hospitals.address: 223 North Van Dien Avenue, Ridgewood, NJ 07450

importantNumbers.label: Bergen County Office of Emergency Management
